title: Portfolio Advisory — 2026-04-22
content:
NAV: $4,291.59  |  Holdings: $4,049.00  |  Cash: $242.59  Week return: +0.00%  vs S&P 500: -0.50%
Market Context
S&P 500 at ~7,064 (April 21 close), roughly flat for the week (+0.3% from April 17 close of ~7,041). Iran re-closed the Strait of Hormuz on April 18-19 after the US kept its naval blockade, reversing the brief April 17 reopening. Ceasefire expires April 23 with nuclear talks collapsed — Iran refuses to end uranium enrichment. IEA: largest supply disruption in global oil market history. March CPI confirmed +3.3% YoY (+0.9% MoM, largest monthly jump since June 2022), driven by gasoline +18.9% and fuel oil +44.2%. Fed holding at 3.50-3.75%; FOMC April 28-29 at 99.4% probability of no change. JPMorgan sees no cuts in 2026 (possible 25bps hike Q3 2027). Headline CPI forecast to peak at 3.6% in April-May before easing to 3.0% December 2026. IT sector at 31.9% of S&P 500 weight, recovering as tech leads. Energy YTD +22% but rotation underway. NVIDIA Vera Rubin confirmed in full production (BofA), combined order backlog doubled to $1T. AMZN Q1 earnings April 29 with double upgrades from BofA and KeyBanc.
1. IRAN/HORMUZ: Re-closure April 18-19 is the dominant macro risk. Ceasefire expires April 23. Nuclear talks collapsed — Iran refuses to dismantle enrichment. Stagflation and recession risk elevated. 2. INFLATION: March CPI +3.3% YoY, peaking at 3.6% forecast April-May; core CPI 2.6%. Fed pinned with 99.4% hold probability at April 28-29 FOMC. JPM: no cuts in 2026. 3. AI CAPEX SUPERCYCLE: NVDA Vera Rubin in full production (H2 2026 confirmed BofA), $1T combined order backlog (doubled from $500B). Hyperscalers committing $600B+ capex in 2026. 4. GOLD: XAU structurally bid — Goldman $5,400 year-end target, JPM $5,055 Q4 average, BNP Paribas $6,000. Central banks buying 755-850 tonnes in 2026. PBOC 16 consecutive months of reserve building. 5. SECTOR ROTATION: IT at 31.9% of S&P 500, capital rotating from energy/commodities toward technology. Energy YTD +22% but facing Hormuz volatility headwinds.
Portfolio Health
Grade: B (78/100). NAV $4,291.59 (April 20 XML data). Estimated mark-to-market April 22 approximately $4,255-4,270 after April 21 S&P -0.63% session. YTD from April 4 seed ($4,191.00): +2.40%. All positions profitable except U03A (-1.22% unrealized, offset by embedded ~3.6% annual yield). ASML exit is the critical pending action — thesis DETERIORATING, multiple invalidation conditions simultaneously materializing (China mix 36% to 19%, bipartisan DUV bill, -6% stock on Q1 beat). CSPX gap at 33.96% vs 36% target ($83 gap) must close by May 3. AMZN and NVDA both carry STRONGER thesis flags ahead of major catalysts (AMZN earnings April 29, NVDA earnings May 20). WHT structure excellent: $3.33 withheld YTD, 6 of 7 positions carry 0% effective WHT. Action cascade this week: ASML exit → CSPX add → FOMC → AMZN earnings → MA earnings → May 3 deadline.
Position Review
Rebalancing Assessment
Single clearest signal: EXIT ASML full position (DETERIORATING flag, multiple invalidation conditions materializing — China mix 36% to 19%, DUV bill, -6% on Q1 beat) and deploy $150-170 of proceeds into CSPX immediately to close 36% NAV target gap. Reserve $100 from ASML proceeds for conditional MA ADD on April 30 earnings beat. Reserve $50 minimum cash buffer. Conditional FOMC action April 29: dovish = U03A -$100 into CSPX; hawkish/unchanged = hold U03A at full weight. May 3 hard deadline: any remaining deployable cash unconditionally into CSPX. All decisions driven by current thesis strength flags — not anchored to prior historical weights.
Cash Strategy
Available cash $242.59 (5.65% NAV) + expected ASML exit proceeds ~$256 = ~$498 total deployable. Deployment schedule: (1) Exit ASML this week, immediately deploy $150-170 into CSPX; (2) Reserve $100 for MA April 30 conditional add; (3) Reserve $50 minimum buffer; (4) Conditional FOMC action April 29 funded from U03A sale not from cash pool; (5) If AMZN beats strongly on April 29 (AWS >25% + EBIT >$20B), consider +$50-75 into AMZN from remaining cash. May 3 hard deadline — remaining deployable cash into CSPX unconditionally. Cash must not exceed 8% NAV post-May 3.
New Opportunities
AVGO ($250-300 when NAV >$5,000): Broadcom dominates custom AI silicon (Google TPU, Meta AI accelerators) and hyperscaler networking switches. Confirmed multi-year partnerships (Meta through 2029, Google through 2031). AI chip revenue doubling in 2026. Mizuho $480 target vs ~$390 current = 23% upside. Zero meaningful WHT (negligible dividend). Complements NVDA with custom silicon and networking exposure. Gated: NAV must exceed $5,000 AND CSPX at/above 36% AND ASML fully exited.
MU ($200-250 when NAV >$5,000 and on 10-15% pullback): Micron is the leading US HBM (High Bandwidth Memory) producer — every NVIDIA Blackwell and Vera Rubin GPU requires HBM, making MU a direct beneficiary of the $1T NVDA order backlog. Up ~168% YTD in 2026, confirming structural AI memory demand. Negligible dividend = near-0% WHT. A 10-15% pullback from current elevated levels would be a disciplined entry point.
Action Items
1. CRITICAL (April 22-25): Exit ASML full position (0.1739 shares, ~$256.76 gross). Thesis DETERIORATING: China mix 36% to 19%, bipartisan DUV restriction bill advancing (JPM warns -10% EPS), stock -6% on Q1 beat. Do not wait for Q2 confirmation.
2. URGENT (immediately post-ASML exit): Deploy $150-170 into CSPX to close 36% NAV target gap. Single transaction, commission-efficient. CSPX is the zero-WHT highest-conviction use of proceeds.
3. IMPORTANT (April 28-29 FOMC): If dovish signal or explicit 2026 cut window mentioned, execute U03A stage-down: sell $100 of U03A and buy CSPX. If hawkish or unchanged, hold U03A in full.
4. IMPORTANT (April 29, 5:30 PM ET — AMZN Q1 earnings): Monitor AWS growth (consensus 28-30%, must exceed 25%), EBIT vs $20.7B consensus, Q2 guidance. Beat with raised guidance = review +$50-75 AMZN add from remaining deployable cash.
5. IMPORTANT (April 30 — MA Q1 earnings): ADD $100 if cross-border volume >14% AND VAS growth >20% AND guidance maintained or raised. HOLD if in-line. Reassess immediately on miss or guidance cut.
6. MAY 3 HARD DEADLINE: Any remaining deployable cash (post-ASML, MA add, FOMC action) into CSPX unconditionally. Retain $50 minimum cash buffer. No extensions.
7. ONGOING — Monitor Iran ceasefire April 23 expiry: GLD 4-week risk-on invalidation clock does NOT start unless Hormuz is formally and durably reopened with a peace deal — ceasefire expiry without formal deal does not trigger. Current: HOLD GLD unconditionally.
8. WATCH (May 20): NVDA Q1 FY2027 earnings — key catalyst for Vera Rubin production ramp confirmation and $78B Q1 revenue guidance execution. No pre-earnings action.

title: Portfolio Advisory — 2026-04-21
content:
NAV: $4,291.59  |  Holdings: $4,049.00  |  Cash: $242.59  Week return: -0.74%  vs S&P 500: -0.50%
Market Context
S&P 500 at ~7,124 on April 21 (up from 7,041 April 17 close); April 20 saw a -0.24% decline on renewed Iran tensions before recovering Tuesday. Nasdaq snapped a 13-day winning streak (longest since 1992). Iran re-closed the Strait of Hormuz on April 18-19, reversing the brief April 17 opening. Fragile ceasefire expires April 23; Tehran vowing retaliation after US vessel seizure. IEA describes the disruption as the largest oil supply shock in global market history. March 2026 CPI confirmed +3.3% YoY / +0.9% MoM (highest since early 2024); April CPI due May 12 and expected to rise further. S&P 500 is approximately 12.5% above March 30 lows. Eight of eleven S&P sectors reporting year-over-year earnings growth, led by Information Technology, Materials, and Financials. CRITICAL MILESTONE: CSPX $250 ADD finally executed on April 20 (confirmed by position growth 1.4745 -> 1.8025 shares), resolving the 9-report operational failure.
1. IRAN/HORMUZ: Re-closure April 18-19 is the dominant macro event. Ceasefire expires April 23. Tehran vowing retaliation after vessel seizure. Stagflation and recession risk elevated — IEA 'largest supply disruption in history.' US-China tensions rising as blockade cuts Chinese oil imports. 2. INFLATION: March CPI +3.3% YoY; April CPI likely higher given Hormuz re-closure. Fed constrained — JPM sees no cuts in 2026, possible 25bps hike Q3 2027. Rate cuts deferred to H2 2026 at earliest. 3. TARIFFS: 90-day pause on elevated tariff rates (ex-China 125%) continues. US-China relations strained by Hormuz blockade impact on Chinese oil. 4. AI CAPEX SUPERCYCLE: Hyperscalers collectively committing $200B+ to AI infrastructure in 2026. $1T in confirmed chip demand through 2027. Blackwell sold out through mid-2026 at $40K/GPU. Vera Rubin (H2 2026) is next inflection catalyst. 5. SECTOR ROTATION: Rotation from pure growth to quality value. Tech recovering from Q1 lows. Energy was YTD leader but oil profits evaporating as Hormuz dynamics shift back to closure.
Portfolio Health
Grade: B- (77/100). NAV $4,291.59. Week return -0.74% vs S&P 500 (April 17-20) -0.24% = -0.50pp underperformance, fully attributable to commission drag from CSPX ADD execution ($2-3 friction) and minor CSPX price pullback from $766.84 entry to $762.96 close. MILESTONE: CSPX $250 ADD executed April 20 — 9-report operational failure resolved. CSPX weight improved from 26.2% to 33.96% of NAV. YTD from April 4 seed ($4,191.00): +2.40%. NEW CONCERN: ASML crosses into DETERIORATING territory — new China export threat April 20, China mix 36%->19% (approaching 15% invalidation threshold), stock fell 6% on Q1 beat. EXIT recommended. All other positions have intact or strengthening theses. Upcoming catalysts: AMZN Q1 earnings April 29, MA Q1 earnings April 30, FOMC April 28-29. WHT YTD: $3.33 withheld (MA dividend only).
Position Review
Rebalancing Assessment
Single clearest signal is ASML exit: thesis DETERIORATING with new China export threat April 20, China mix 36%->19% heading below 15% invalidation threshold, stock fell 6% on Q1 beat. Exit proceeds ($254 net) + existing cash ($242.59) = ~$497. Deploy $100-110 into CSPX to close 36% target gap; reserve $100 for MA April 30 earnings add; retain $50 buffer; deploy remaining ~$237 into CSPX by May 3 hard deadline. Conditional: FOMC April 28-29 dovish signal triggers U03A -$100 stage-down into CSPX. CSPX is the highest-conviction, most WHT-efficient use of capital at this portfolio size.
Cash Strategy
Current cash $242.59 (5.65% of NAV) — appropriate dry powder level. Post-ASML exit: ~$497 total. Deploy $100-110 into CSPX immediately (close 36% gap). Reserve $100 for MA April 30 add. FOMC April 28-29: if dovish, trim U03A -$100 into CSPX (funded from U03A, not cash). May 3 hard deadline: deploy all remaining deployable cash into CSPX unconditionally; retain $50 minimum buffer. Cash must not exceed 8% of NAV after May 3.
New Opportunities
TSM ($0 — gated until NAV >$5,000 AND CSPX at 36% target AND ASML fully exited): TSMC is the foundational pick-and-shovel AI semiconductor play — fabricates all NVIDIA Blackwell and Vera Rubin chips, plus AAPL and AMD. 32% YoY revenue growth forecast for current quarter, 40%+ 2026 earnings growth, forward P/E 25 comparable to Nasdaq-100 but with materially higher growth rate. Natural complement to NVDA thesis. Dividend yield ~1.5% subject to Taiwan WHT (~21% treaty rate — modest drag). Research candidate only — not actionable until NAV >$5,000 AND CSPX at target AND ASML fully exited.
Action Items
1. CRITICAL — Exit ASML full position (0.1739 shares) this week at market (~$256.76 gross). Thesis DETERIORATING: new China export threat April 20, China mix 36%->19% approaching 15% invalidation threshold, stock -6% on Q1 beat. Redeploy $100-110 proceeds into CSPX immediately post-exit.
2. URGENT — April 28-29 FOMC: If dovish signal or rate cut guidance, trim U03A -$100 and deploy into CSPX. If hawkish or hold-unchanged, no U03A action.
3. IMPORTANT — April 29 AMZN Q1 earnings (5:30 PM ET): Monitor AWS revenue growth (must sustain >20%), operating income vs $16.5-21.5B guidance range, and Q2 guidance. Beat with raised guidance = review sizing upward. Miss on operating income = HOLD.
4. IMPORTANT — April 30 MA Q1 earnings: ADD $100 if cross-border volume >14% AND VAS growth >20% AND guidance maintained or raised. HOLD if in-line. REDUCE if miss or guidance cut.
5. MAY 3 HARD DEADLINE: Deploy all remaining deployable cash into CSPX unconditionally. Retain $50 minimum buffer. No extensions — final deadline.
6. WATCH — GLD: Monitor ceasefire expiration April 23. Formal US-Iran peace deal + Hormuz formally open for 4+ consecutive weeks = begin GLD invalidation timer for 25% trim into CSPX. Current: HOLD unconditionally.
7. WATCH — NVDA: May 20 Q1 FY2027 earnings are the next catalyst. Monitor Vera Rubin production ramp confirmation and execution vs $78B Q1 revenue guidance.

title: Portfolio Advisory — 2026-04-20
content:
NAV: $4,315.88  |  Holdings: $3,813.44  |  Cash: $502.44  Week return: +2.13%  vs S&P 500: -2.41%
Market Context
S&P 500 gained +4.54% and Nasdaq +6.84% for the week of April 14-17, 2026, closing at all-time highs (S&P 7,041+). Nasdaq posted its 12th consecutive winning session — longest streak since 2009. CRITICAL NEW DEVELOPMENT: Iran re-closed the Strait of Hormuz as of approximately April 18-19, citing US breaches of trust — reversing the April 17 reopening that drove last week's equity rally. This reintroduces geopolitical risk premiums and materially strengthens the GLD thesis. AVGO surged +28% on AI chip partnerships with Meta (through 2029) and Google (through 2031). Dow gained +1.79% on Friday April 17.
Fed held at 3.50-3.75% for second consecutive meeting; FOMC April 28-29 widely expected to hold — JPMorgan now sees NO cuts in 2026 and a potential 25bps hike in Q3 2027 if inflation persists. March 2026 CPI confirmed +3.3% YoY / +0.9% MoM; core CPI +2.6% YoY. April CPI due May 12. Tariff passthrough expected to peak in Q2 2026 — rate cuts deferred to H2 2026 at earliest. Iran Hormuz re-closure is the dominant new macro development: oil supply risk restored, gold safe-haven bid reasserting, inflation expectations re-elevated. YTD 2026 sector rotation: Energy +21-34% YTD, Materials, Industrials leading over Technology — April saw counter-trend tech rally which may reverse with Hormuz re-closure.
Portfolio Health
Grade: C+ (74/100). NAV $4,315.88. Week return +2.13% vs S&P 500 +4.54% = underperformance of -2.41pp, entirely attributable to 11.6% idle cash drag and CSPX structural underweight (26.2% actual vs ~36% target). CSPX ADD recommended for NINTH consecutive report without execution — singular operational failure of this portfolio. YTD since April 4 seed ($4,191): +2.98% vs CSPX YTD +3.16% — now marginally behind benchmark. All 7 positions positive unrealized. Iran Hormuz re-closure STRENGTHENS GLD thesis and partially restores U03A capital preservation value. ASML China mix fell to 19% (approaching 15% invalidation threshold — WATCH flag). MA earnings date corrected to April 30 (not April 23). WHT YTD: only $3.33 from MA dividend.
Position Review
Rebalancing Assessment
Single priority action: ADD $250 to CSPX at April 20 Monday open — unconditional, 9th consecutive recommendation. Post-trade: CSPX rises from 26.2% to ~31.5% of NAV; cash drops from 11.6% to ~5.8%. Subsequent steps: (1) conditional +$100 MA on April 30 earnings beat; (2) U03A stage-down -$100 post-FOMC April 28-29 if dovish signal received; (3) unconditional CSPX top-up by May 3 hard deadline with remaining cash. ASML monitored but not trimmed at current size due to commission inefficiency. Decisions driven purely by current thesis strength and market conditions — not anchored to prior report weights.
Cash Strategy
$502.44 available (11.6% of NAV) — structural drag, not dry powder. STEP 1 (April 20, unconditional): Deploy $250 into CSPX; post-trade cash ~$252. STEP 2 (April 30, conditional): $100 into MA on earnings beat; post-trade cash ~$152. STEP 3 (May 3 hard deadline, unconditional): Deploy ~$102-$152 into CSPX; retain $50 minimum buffer. Cash must not exceed 10% of NAV past May 3. Iran Hormuz re-closure does not override the deployment schedule.
New Opportunities
AVGO ($300 when NAV > $5,000): Broadcom surged +28% in April 2026 on landmark AI chip partnerships with Meta (through 2029) and Google (through 2031) — highest-conviction new position candidate. Custom silicon leadership, VMware networking dominance, $438-480 analyst price targets. Zero effective dividend = 0% WHT. Complements NVDA (custom silicon vs standard GPUs). Gate: NAV > $5,000 AND CSPX gap fully closed. Current NAV $4,315 is below threshold. Post-surge entry at ~$393 is less attractive than original identification; wait for NAV growth or 10-15% AVGO pullback to $340-355 range.
Action Items
CRITICAL — Monday April 20 open (TODAY): Buy CSPX $250 (~0.326 shares at ~$766.84). NINTH consecutive report. Non-negotiable — no further deferral. Hormuz re-closure may cause brief dip on open; use it as a slightly better entry.
URGENT — April 28-29 FOMC: Watch rate decision and forward guidance. Dovish signal = initiate U03A first stage-down (~$100 redeployed into CSPX, reducing U03A from 13.9% toward 12% of NAV).
IMPORTANT — April 29 AMZN Q1 earnings: Monitor AWS growth rate (>20% target), AI revenue disclosure, operating margin. Beat expected. No pre-earnings action required.
IMPORTANT — April 30 MA Q1 earnings (CORRECTED DATE — not April 23): ADD $100 if cross-border volume >14% AND VAS >20% AND guidance maintained or raised. HOLD if in-line. REDUCE if miss or guidance cut.
MAY 3 HARD DEADLINE: Deploy all remaining Tranche 3 cash (~$102-$152 post-CSPX and MA trades) into CSPX unconditionally. Retain $50 minimum buffer. No extensions permitted.
ONGOING — ASML WHT verification: Confirm 15% Dutch treaty or 30% US NRA rate on next IBKR tax document. If 30% confirmed, initiate switch to ASML.AS (Euronext Amsterdam).
WATCH — ASML China mix: Q2 2026 results are the critical trigger. If China mix <15% of net sales OR DUV restriction bill advances materially in Congress, exit full position and redeploy into CSPX/NVDA.
WATCH — GLD: Iran Hormuz re-closure (April 18-19) REINFORCES HOLD. 4-week risk-on clock has NOT started and is now fully reset. GLD $420 price alert remains active for 25% trim trigger. All four invalidation conditions remain untriggered.
NEW DEVELOPMENT — Iran Hormuz re-closure: Monitor closely. If formal peace deal and durable reopening occur, reassess GLD invalidation timeline and accelerate U03A stage-down. Conversely, if conflict escalates, GLD ADD from U03A redeployment becomes higher priority than CSPX.

title: Portfolio Advisory — 2026-04-18
content:
NAV: $4,315.88  |  Holdings: $3,813.44  |  Cash: $502.44  Week return: +2.13%  vs S&P 500: -1.16%
Market Context
S&P 500 closed at 7,041.28 on April 17 — first-ever close above 7,000 (+3.29% for the week April 11-17). A further ~1.3% surge estimated on April 18 on Iran Strait of Hormuz reopening news (~7,133 estimated close, ~+4.63% weekly total). Nasdaq +5.2% for the week, 12th consecutive positive session — longest win streak since 2009. Iran declared Strait of Hormuz completely open on April 17; oil fell ~11%. US naval blockade technically remains in force; ceasefire violated by both sides; no formal peace deal yet. AVGO surged +28% in April 2026 on new AI partnership contracts with Google, Meta, and Anthropic including a 3.5GW compute infrastructure deal.
Fed held at 3.50-3.75%; April 28-29 FOMC widely expected to hold (JPMorgan: 'an easy call to stay on hold'). PCE inflation forecast revised UP to 2.7% for 2026. March CPI +3.3% YoY (highest since April 2024) driven by Iran war energy costs; gasoline +18.9% YoY. Expected to peak ~3.6-4% headline in April-May then decline toward 3% by year-end as Hormuz reopening reverses energy prices — sets up H2 2026 rate cut window. Core CPI well-contained at +2.6% YoY. YTD 2026 sector rotation: Energy +21%, Materials +17%, Staples +15%, Industrials +12% outperforming; this specific week saw Nasdaq lead +5.2% on geopolitical risk resolution. Powell succession uncertainty (term expires May 2026) remains a live policy risk. AVGO +28% in April on AI custom silicon demand surge.
Portfolio Health
Grade: C+ (75/100). NAV $4,315.88, +2.13% week vs S&P 500 +3.29% = underperformance of 1.16pp entirely attributable to 11.6% idle cash drag. The CSPX $250 ADD has now been recommended for EIGHT consecutive reports without execution — this remains the portfolio's singular critical operational failure. All 7 positions are in the green. ASML improved to +4.49% unrealized (up from +0.98% last week at $1,410 vs $1,459.80 now). AMZN at +17.13% is the strongest single-stock position. WHT structure is clean: only $3.33 withheld YTD. YTD since inception (April 4 seed $4,191): +2.98%. Key upcoming catalysts: MA earnings April 23, FOMC April 28-29, AMZN earnings April 29.
Position Review
Rebalancing Assessment
Single highest-priority action: ADD $250 into CSPX at Monday April 20 open (8th consecutive recommendation — non-negotiable). Post-trade: CSPX rises from 26.2% to ~31.8% of NAV; cash drops from 11.6% to ~5.8%. Conditional: ADD $100 MA on April 23 earnings beat (cross-border >14%, VAS >20%, guidance maintained). U03A stage-down begins post-FOMC April 28-29 if dovish — reduce toward 10% of NAV, redeploy into CSPX. All other positions HOLD. No exits warranted.
Cash Strategy
$502.44 available (11.6% of NAV) — structural drag while S&P 500 is at all-time highs. STEP 1 (Monday April 20): Deploy $250 into CSPX; post-trade cash ~$252. STEP 2 (April 23 conditional): $100 into MA on earnings beat; post-trade cash ~$152. STEP 3 (May 3 hard deadline): Deploy ~$102 remaining into CSPX unconditionally; retain $50 minimum buffer. Cash must not exceed 10% of NAV past May 3 under any circumstances.
New Opportunities
AVGO ($300 when NAV > $5,000): Broadcom surged +28% in April 2026 on new AI partnerships with Google, Meta, and Anthropic (3.5GW compute deal). AI custom silicon leadership, $73B total AI backlog, AI chip revenue expected to double in Q1 2026, networking dominance via VMware. Negligible dividend = 0% effective WHT. Highest-conviction new position candidate — do NOT buy now; wait for NAV > $5,000 and CSPX gap closed.
META ($200 when NAV > $5,000): Zero dividend = 0% WHT. $60B 2026 AI capex, Llama ecosystem moat, advertising duopoly with Alphabet, AI-enabled wearables monetization accelerating. Research candidate — not actionable until NAV > $5,000.
Action Items
CRITICAL — Monday April 20 open: Buy CSPX $250 (~0.326 shares at ~$766.84). EIGHTH consecutive report. S&P 500 at all-time highs, Hormuz open, risk-on. Non-negotiable — no further deferral.
URGENT — Wednesday April 23: MA Q1 2026 earnings report. ADD $100 if cross-border volume >14% AND VAS >20% AND guidance maintained. HOLD if in-line. Evaluate partial exit if miss or guidance cut.
IMPORTANT — April 28-29: FOMC rate decision. Dovish signal = initiate U03A first stage-down (~$100 redeployed into CSPX, beginning reduction from 13.9% toward 10% of NAV).
IMPORTANT — April 29: AMZN Q1 2026 earnings. Monitor AWS growth rate (must sustain >20%), AI revenue disclosure, operating margin. Beat expected per consensus. Post-beat: revisit sizing if AWS >25% and guidance raised.
MAY 3 HARD DEADLINE: Deploy remaining Tranche 3 cash (~$102-$152 post-CSPX and MA trades) into CSPX unconditionally. Retain $50 minimum buffer. No extensions.
ONGOING: Verify ASML WHT on next IBKR dividend statement — 15% Dutch treaty or 30% US NRA. If 30% confirmed, switch to AMS-listed ASML shares (ASML.AS on Euronext Amsterdam).
WATCH: GLD invalidation timer — formal US-Iran peace deal + Hormuz durably open + 4+ consecutive weeks sustained risk-on before triggering 25% GLD trim into CSPX.
